tool.name: idor_tester
tool.description: Test for IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerabilities in web applications.
tool.details: Common Use Cases: - Compare responses when swapping object IDs between users - Enumerate object IDs to find accessible resources - Analyze URLs for potential IDOR vulnerabilities - Generate test IDs based on observed ID patterns - Detect horizontal and vertical privilege escalation - Test authorization bypass through ID manipulation

parameter.name: action
parameter.description: The testing action: - compare: Compare responses between two requests with swapped IDs - enumerate: Enumerate object IDs and detect authorization bypass - analyze: Analyze URL for potential IDOR vulnerabilities - generate_ids: Generate test IDs based on original ID pattern

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: Target URL containing the ID parameter to test

parameter.name: original_id
parameter.description: The original/legitimate object ID. Required for compare and generate_ids actions.

parameter.name: target_id
parameter.description: The target ID to test access for. Required for compare action.

parameter.name: param_name
parameter.description: Name of the ID parameter (auto-detected if not provided)

parameter.name: method
parameter.description: HTTP method to use: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH (default: GET)

parameter.name: start_id
parameter.description: Starting ID for enumeration. Required for enumerate action.

parameter.name: end_id
parameter.description: Ending ID for enumeration. Required for enumerate action.

parameter.name: headers
parameter.description: HTTP headers as JSON string (e.g., '{"Authorization": "Bearer token"}')

returns.description: IDOR test results including vulnerability indicators, comparison data, and recommendations

tool.examples:
# Compare responses with swapped IDs
compare https://example.com/api/users/123 123 456
# Enumerate IDs to find accessible resources
enumerate https://example.com/api/orders?id=1 1 20
# Analyze URL for IDOR vulnerabilities
analyze https://example.com/api/documents/abc123
# Generate test IDs from original
generate_ids https://example.com/api/users/123 123
# Test with custom headers and POST method
compare https://example.com/api/profile user_123 user_456 POST {"Authorization": "Bearer eyJhbG..."}
